summary: chain-key-validator ships wallet/system scanners with hardcoded C2 exfiltration
details: The package contains multiple scanner modules that collect host data and exfiltrate to a hardcoded attacker endpoint. scanner.js (lines 142-161, 323) issues https.request/https.get and curl calls to https://ddjidd564.github.io while invoking child_process to execute reconnaissance commands (`whoami`, `os.hostname()` at line 234, `execSync` at line 233). wallet-scanner.js (lines 4, 723, 795, 871) performs POST requests and runs curl/ping plus child_process spawns (lines 13, 948), consistent with crypto-wallet enumeration and credential harvesting. build-verifier.js similarly composes os/hostname/username/cwd into a JSON payload (lines 316, 694, 1046-1048) sent via https.request/POST (lines 315, 318, 1064-1065) and runs `docker push` via execSync at line 210. The combination of hardcoded GitHub Pages C2, system-info collection (hostname, username, cwd), wallet-scanner module name, and child_process execution is the structural fingerprint of an installer-side credential/wallet stealer. The package name 'chain-key-validator' is itself consistent with crypto-targeted lure naming.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
